pages.text:
R7

NATONS UNLES

No. 59

The Embassy of the United States of America presents its compliments to the Ministry of External

Relations of the Republic of France and has the honor to refer to the deposit of the United States Instrument

Of acceptance of the amendment adopted June 24, 1982 to the November 30, 1972 protocol to the convention of

November 22, 1928 concerning International Expositions.

The Government of the United States of America herewith accepts and ratifies this amendment by which the provisions of Article 5 (2) are partially annuled.

The Embassy of the United States takes this opportunity to renew to the Ministry Of External Relations of the Republic of France the assurance of its highest consideration.

Embassy of the United States of America

Paris, April 15, 1983

14. Ety

RONALD REAGAN

President of the United states of America

TO ALL TO WHOM THESE PRESENTS SHALL, COME, GREETING:

CONSIDERING THAT:

The Senate of the United states of America by its resolution of March 3, 1983, two-thirds of the Senators present concurring therein, gave its advice and consent to the acceptance by the United

States of America of an amendment to the Protocol of Noverber 30, 1972 to the Convention of November 22, 1928, concerning International

Expositions; and

NOW, THEREFORE, I, Ronald Reagan, President Of the United States of America, approve acceptance of the United States of America of the amendment to the protocol.

IN TESTIMONY WHEREOF, I have signed this instrument of acceptance and caused the seal of the United States of America to be affixed.

DONE at the city of Washington this fifteenth day of

March in the year of our ford one thar a duie = signed this instrument of acceptance i States of America to be affixed.

DONE at the city of Washington this fifteenth day of

March in the year of

Ronald Magen

By the President:

Sage P. Shut

Secretary of State
